messages.content: Man, I still can't believe it. Kaszek actually said yes verbally yesterday. Like... feels surreal, you know?
That's incredible news! After all the work you've put in, how are you feeling about it?

Honestly? I'm still processing. Like, part of me wants to celebrate but another part is like... don't jinx it until the term sheet is signed. My girlfriend has been so supportive through all this craziness though. She's the one pushing me to actually enjoy this moment.
She sounds wise. It's important to acknowledge these wins, even if the paperwork isn't final yet. What's your plan if everything goes through?

First thing? We're planning a trip to Cartagena if this term sheet actually lands. Like, a real vacation. Haven't taken more than a weekend off since... God, probably since we started hitting those growth numbers back in October.
That sounds perfect. You've definitely earned it. How does it feel looking back on this whole journey?

Wild, honestly. Like, remember when I was sleeping five hours a night during that hiring and fundraising crunch? Now we're at 78K MRR, churn is down to 2.4%, and Lucia's handling the backend stuff so well. Sometimes I just sit back and think... how did we actually pull this off?
It's the result of all those small decisions and late nights adding up. The growth trajectory speaks for itself - from 45K to 78K in just a few months.

Yeah, and the team... man, hiring Lucia was such a game changer. I can actually delegate now instead of trying to code everything myself at 2am. It's funny how reflecting on the journey makes you realize how much has actually changed, even when you're in the middle of it.
That's the beauty of having the right people in place. It sounds like you've built something really solid, regardless of what happens with the term sheet.

True. I mean, even if Kaszek somehow backs out - which I really hope they don't - we've proven we can hit those growth targets they wanted. 20% month over month felt impossible back then, but here we are.

summary: Carlos shares his surreal feeling about Kaszek's verbal commitment and reflects on the fundraising journey. He discusses planning a Cartagena trip with his supportive girlfriend and looks back on how the team and metrics have transformed since the intense growth period.
